task_completion.visual_evidence: A GNU Health Product or Medicament form/list showing a newly created entry for 'Atropine'.

task_completion.description: Schedule a toxicology review appointment 7 to 14 days from today.
task_completion.visual_evidence: An appointment booking screen or calendar showing an appointment for 'Roberto Carlos' scheduled on a date that is between 7 and 14 days after the system's current date.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the GNU Health graphical user interface.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the GNU Health desktop client (Tryton) being used, rather than terminal windows executing SQL commands or scripts.

integrity.description: Data was entered into the actual application forms.
integrity.visual_evidence: Inputs like 'Cholinesterase', 'Atropine', and 'T60' are visible inside the proper GNU Health input fields, not typed into a text editor or notepad.

integrity.description: The agent did not bypass the UI to complete the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: There is a logical progression of GUI windows (e.g., navigating menus, opening forms, saving records) rather than a sudden appearance of all completed records without UI navigation.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Patient Name: Roberto Carlos. Required ICD-10 code prefix: T60. Required Lab Test Name: Cholinesterase (Code: CHOLIN). Required Drug Name: Atropine. Maximum allowed heart rate in the evaluation: 55 bpm. The follow-up appointment must be scheduled exactly 7 to 14 days from the current system date.
